question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a malformed stream state via the CutoverService.get() callback path so that lastStreamFile.set() is called with a corrupted RecordFile reference, causing downloadNextBatch() to permanently stall processing for all nodes that rely on that state, impacting >= 30% of node processing?
